headline_summary: UN Security Council scheduled a vote on a Bahrain-drafted resolution on protecting commercial shipping around the Strait of Hormuz; China opposed any authorization of force (Reuters, 2026-04-02).
UK hosted virtual talks with about 35 countries on options to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, including minesweeping and tanker protection (Reuters, 2026-04-02).
Iran gave the Philippines assurances of safe passage for Philippine-flagged vessels and energy cargoes through the Strait of Hormuz (Reuters, 2026-04-02).
IEA chief warned Middle East oil supply disruptions were expected to rise in April and hit Europe, reinforcing upside risk to crude and shipping costs (Reuters, 2026-04-01).
US LNG exports hit record highs as buyers sought to replace Middle East supplies amid the disruption (Reuters, 2026-04-01).
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz access/security uncertainty and timeline for reopening
UN mandate/use-of-force disagreement among major powers
Elevated war-risk insurance, rerouting, and shipping capacity constraints
Oil and gas supply displacement (Europe/Asia) increasing price volatility
Potential for escalation affecting energy and maritime assets

Energy: Upside and volatility risk in crude while Hormuz security and clearance operations remain uncertain.
Higher freight/insurance costs can tighten refined products and LNG differentials; replacement flows (e.g., higher US exports) only partially offset disruption.
FX: Risk-off moves tend to support USD and pressure energy-importing EM FX via higher import bills and transport costs.
GCC pegs limit spot FX moves but prolonged disruption can transmit through liquidity conditions and credit risk.
Rates: Energy-driven inflation uncertainty can lift breakevens/term premia even as safe-haven demand can compress core yields during acute escalation.
Credit spreads may widen for EM sovereigns and for transport/industrials exposed to higher fuel and shipping costs.
Equities: Energy and defense may outperform; airlines/shipping-intensive sectors face margin headwinds.
Higher geopolitical risk premium can favor defensives and volatility strategies; EM equities remain sensitive to oil and USD strength.

sources.title: UN to vote on Hormuz resolution as China opposes authorization of force
UK to host talks with 35 countries on reopening Strait of Hormuz
Iran to allow safe passage of Philippine ships, fuel supply through Strait of Hormuz, says Manila
Middle East oil supply disruptions to rise in April and hit Europe, IEA chief says
US LNG exports break record high as Middle East war disrupts global supply
